label: 1 CE
era_summary: Pax Romana under Augustus. Han dynasty in China at its peak before the Xin interregnum. Massive trade networks: Silk Road, Indian Ocean monsoon trade.

rome_italy.summary: Italian core under Augustus. Pax Romana, urban water + grain dole, low banditry, no major civil war since 31 BCE.

sources.label: Maddison Project: Roman GDP per capita estimates

han_china.summary: Former Han dynasty mature, granary system, civil exams nascent. Trouble brewing — Wang Mang regency ~6 CE leads to usurpation in 9 CE.

gupta_india.summary: Pre-Gupta. Fragmented post-Mauryan polities; Kushans rising in north.

persia.summary: Parthian Arsacid empire, periodic Roman wars (e.g. Crassus 53 BCE recent memory). Stable centrally, contested west.

egypt_nile.summary: Roman province since 30 BCE. Grain heartland, predictable Nile flood, urban Alexandria.

constantinople_balkans.summary: Roman province, but frontier. Dacian raids, less urbanized than Italy.

andalusia_iberia.summary: Roman Hispania, post-Cantabrian wars peace. Mining + agricultural wealth.

francia.summary: Gaul, Romanized but frontier. Germanic pressure across Rhine.

england.summary: Pre-Roman Britain. Tribal, no central authority, low urbanization.

scandinavia.summary: Iron Age tribal societies, harsh climate, low food security.

rus.summary: Sparsely settled, no states. Pre-Slavic migrations underway.

central_asia.summary: Silk Road oases (Sogdiana, Bactria) — wealthy trade nodes, Yuezhi/Kushan rising.

japan.summary: Yayoi period, agrarian villages, no unified state.

mesoamerica.summary: Late Preclassic Maya cities expanding, El Mirador peak. Stable.

andes.summary: Late Chavín / early Moche. Coastal valley polities, stable.

sahel_west_africa.summary: Iron Age Nok culture fading. Pre-Ghana empire, mostly stateless.

ethiopia.summary: Pre-Aksumite Da'amat / proto-Aksum. Long-distance Red Sea trade.

north_america_east.summary: Hopewell tradition — earthworks, foraging + early horticulture. Low density.
